assertions.text: Vehicle was traveling just under 80 km/h on the highway.
Driver was easing off the throttle (decelerating) as traffic slowed.
There was a single impact (one jolt only).
The car dropped to a crawl within a couple of seconds after the impact.
No explicit statement of braking; driver eased off rather than applied brakes (no claim of active braking).
The impact reduced the vehicle speed from near 80 km/h to a crawl within a couple of seconds (implying a severe speed reduction).

verdicts.evidence: The GPS speed samples show the vehicle at [79.0 km/h at t=0.0](T4) and gradually decreasing; the first sample is [79.0](T2) and the max recorded is [79.0 km/h](T2), consistent with "just under 80 km/h".

The GPS speed trend shows a gradual decline from [79.0 km/h at t=0.0 to 70.0 km/h at t=8.0](T4) before the impact window, and detect_events reports a sustained deceleration segment on accel_x from [t=8.15 to t=9.92](T3) indicating easing off rather than an abrupt brake.

detect_events found two distinct impact spikes on accel_x at [t=9.20](T3) and [t=10.17](T3) (magnitudes [4.7 g](T3) and [5.75 g](T3)), so telemetry shows at least two jolts rather than a single impact.

GPS speed dropped from [70.0 km/h at t=8.0] to [43.0 km/h at t=11.0] per detect_events (a [27.0 km/h drop](T3)), and the gps samples show [58.0 km/h at t=10.0] and [43.0 km/h at t=11.0](T4), indicating the vehicle slowed to a crawl (around [12.0 km/h later at t=15-16](T4)) within a couple of seconds after the impact spike at [t=10.17](T3).

There is no brake-channel data in the available telemetry; GPS and accel show deceleration (gps drop from [79.0 to 70.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=8.0](T4) and an accel_x sustained segment [t=8.15 to t=9.92](T3)), but that cannot distinguish easing off the throttle from active brake application.

Integrated accel on accel_x estimates a delta-v of [−67.8 km/h](T2) over the window and GPS shows speed change from [79.0 km/h at t=0.0 to 12.0 km/h at t=16.0] (a [67.0 km/h change](T2)), and detect_events documents a fast speed drop of [27.0 km/h between t=8.0 and t=11.0](T3), supporting that the impact coincided with a large reduction in speed to near-crawl within seconds.

result.rationale: Telemetry shows initial speed near [79.0 km/h](T2/T4), sustained deceleration leading into clearly detected impact spikes at [t=9.20 and t=10.17](T3) and a rapid GPS speed drop to [43.0 km/h by t=11.0](T3) and to [~12.0 km/h by t=16.0](T4), supporting most of the narrative except the claim of a single impact which is contradicted by two detected impact spikes.

citations.claim: 79.0 km/h at t=0.0 to 70.0 km/h at t=8.0
79.0 to 70.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=8.0

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented
rollover-suspect if on a roll/pitch axis; axis frame undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
